athlete.name: Julian
athlete.week_start_day: floating — based on last rest day

training_phase.name: Sprint Development
training_phase.description: Build neuromuscular top-end speed. SWOLF has plateaued at 35-36 for 5 months on aerobic volume alone. Shift emphasis: 2+ sprint blocks per week, shorter sessions (1600-1800m for sprint days), full rest between reps. Maintain technique and threshold work as secondary.

phase_progression.name: Speed Integration
phase_progression.focus: Race-pace reps, turn sharpening, lactate tolerance

rolling_bests.best_25m_sprint_protocol_note: INT 20, sprint finish, post-cramp and post-threshold fatigue. Previous best 17.0s (session 15, post-800m). Also now the clean sprint best — previous clean best was 17.4s (session 16).
rolling_bests.best_25m_unverified_note: INT 15.1 on 2026-05-15 recorded 16.7s but adjacent Garmin glitch (INT 14) makes timing unreliable. Not counted as official best.

dryland_baselines.pull_ups_total_4_sets_note: Session 14 baseline (park bar, fresh to pull-ups). Session 18 total = 17 — NOT a regression. Two confounders: slippery bars + inverted row pre-fatigue. Retest needed: dry bar, pull-ups before inverted rows.
dryland_baselines.v_ups_session18_note: 31 total (13/10/8) vs 29 baseline (15/8/6) — genuine improvement. Session 14 under heavy fatigue, session 18 fresher.
dryland_baselines.dead_hang_session18_note: 30/30/20s — session 18 results compromised by slippery bars. Not a valid comparison.
dryland_baselines.l_sit_hold_note: First baseline. Set 3 stopped early — right quad pre-cramp. Keep sets at 10-12s and progress slowly.
dryland_baselines.bar_hang_external_rotation: NOT YET ESTABLISHED — skipped session 18 (slippery bars). Carry to next dryland.
dryland_baselines.context: Session 14 baselines under swim fatigue (day after 9/10 sprint session). Session 18 at calisthenic park — slippery bars compromised grip-dependent results.

rolling_bests.notes: Best 25m raw = 16.1s (Apr 10, unrested mid-threshold set). Sprint protocol best = 16.8s (May 18, INT 20, post-cramp post-threshold). Previous protocol best 17.0s (May 13). Clean sprint best also now 16.8s — previous 17.4s (May 15). Sprint SWOLF: 24 (best, first achieved May 15, matched May 18). Avg pace best: 1:27 (May 15).

swolf_trend.summary: Session avg SWOLF hit 31 (May 15) — all-time best. Sprint SWOLF improved to 24 — consecutive sprint sessions produced new sprint SWOLF bests (26→25→24). Trend is clearly downward and accelerating.

history.note: Garmin error / different pool — outlier, exclude
First structured sprint block. Sprint SWOLF 26-27.
Technique session. Sprint SWOLF 25 (new best at time).
NEW SESSION BEST. Sprint SWOLF 24 — NEW SPRINT BEST. Avg pace 1:27 — NEW PACE BEST.

Threshold session, 1,950m. Left quad cramp at rep 6 of main set. Sprint finish completed post-cramp: NEW SPRINT PROTOCOL BEST 16.8s (INT 20). Sprint SWOLF 24 matched. SWOLF 34 consistent with threshold baseline.

weekly_block_tracking.description: A block = 3 pool + 1 dryland, fulfilled in any order. Blocks do NOT reset on calendar days.
weekly_block_tracking.note: Block 2 in progress. Session 1 (Threshold pool) complete. Session 2 (Dryland) complete. Sessions 3 (Sprint pool) and 4 (Technique pool) remaining. Right quad pre-cramp flagged in dryland — monitor in next pool session.

block_2_plan.equipment_note: calisthenic park — bars only (originally planned rings only — equipment clarified)

blocks.name: Drill Block
blocks.content: 4×25m wall push-off activation + 4×25m fingertip drag drill

targets.stroke_count: 9/length, flag if drifting to 10+ in reps 6-8

dryland.context: Block 2 dryland. Calisthenic park — bars only. Slippery bars throughout — significantly impacted all grip-dependent exercises. Inverted rows placed before pull-ups (more swim-specific order) — creates pre-fatigue on pull-up totals vs session 14 where pull-ups were done fresh.

exercises.name: Pull-Ups
exercises.note: Baseline 22 (session 14). Drop not a regression — two confounders present.

exercises.name: Bar-Assisted Deep Squat Hold
exercises.note: All sets complete — no quad issues

exercises.name: Bar-Assisted Single-Leg Squat
exercises.note: All sets complete — no left quad issues

right_quad_pre_cramp.implication: Bilateral quad/hip flexor deficit confirmed. Not isolated to left leg (session 17). General hip flexor endurance under sustained isometric load is the issue. Manage L-sit progression carefully — keep sets at 10-12s, do not push range until baseline is stable across 3 full sets.

coach_flags: NEW RIGHT QUAD PRE-CRAMP FLAG — L-sit set 3. Different leg and different exercise from session 17 left quad cramp. Confirms bilateral hip flexor endurance deficit, not a one-sided issue.
INVERTED ROWS — new baseline established: 4×12 clean all sets. Strong first session.
PULL-UPS: 17 total vs 22 baseline — conditions compromised (slippery bars + inverted row pre-fatigue). NOT a regression. Retest needed on dry bar with pull-ups performed before inverted rows.
DEAD HANG: results invalid today — slippery bars. Retest dry bar.
V-UPS: 31 total (13/10/8) — genuine improvement on 29 baseline (15/8/6). Session 14 was under heavy fatigue; session 18 was fresher.
HOLLOW BODY: 3×20s matched. Target 25s next dryland.
L-SIT BASELINE: 12/11/10s — first baseline established. Set 3 stopped due to right quad pre-cramp.
BAR HANG EXTERNAL ROTATION: skipped for safety (slippery bars). Carry to next dryland — not yet replaced lat pullover in practice.
BLOCK D: fully completed — left quad held throughout all three exercises. Positive sign for wall push-off recovery trajectory.
sessions.notes: Block 2, Session 2. Dryland at calisthenic park, bars only. Slippery bars compromised grip-dependent exercises. Equipment was calisthenic park (not rings as originally planned — catalogue note corrected). Inverted rows before pull-ups is the correct swim-specific order but pre-fatigues pull-up totals — keep this order going forward and note for comparisons. Right quad pre-cramp in L-sit set 3 confirms bilateral hip flexor endurance deficit. Block D completed without left quad recurrence.

sessions.distance_note: Session terminated at main set rep 6 (50m into 200m) due to left quad cramp. 5 full 200m reps completed. Sprint finish and cool-down completed after cramp eased during extended rest.

metrics.avg_pace_note: Calculated from moving time 30:49 ÷ 1,950m. Garmin avg pace field blank due to mixed interval structure including drills.
metrics.best_25m_split_context: INT 20, sprint finish, post-cramp and post-threshold fatigue. NEW SPRINT PROTOCOL BEST.
metrics.max_hr_note: Garmin optical HR spike in INT 15 (mid-main set). Physiologically implausible — discard. True max likely ~175 bpm.
metrics.drill_push_off_note: Pure push-off glide lengths, 0 strokes. Best glide: 12.9s (INT 5).
metrics.cool_down_note: Max HR 175 in cool-down — CO2 tolerance still lagging.

left_quad_cramp.onset: 6th 200m rep (INT 18), 50m into rep — 2 lengths completed then cramp
left_quad_cramp.trigger: Wall push-off and dolphin kick motion during threshold reps
left_quad_cramp.recovery: 2:19 extended rest — cramp eased enough to complete sprint finish and cool-down
left_quad_cramp.sprint_finish_observation: 6×25m sprint reps completed post-cramp with 2min+ rest between each. Cramp did not recur, suggesting longer rest between efforts allows quad recovery.
left_quad_cramp.implication: Quad/hip flexor chain undertrained relative to current swimming demand. Dryland Block 2 must build controlled quad base before any explosive plyometric work.

coach_flags: NEW SPRINT PROTOCOL BEST: 16.8s (INT 20, sprint finish) — previous 17.0s (session 15). Achieved post-cramp, post-threshold fatigue.
NEW CLEAN SPRINT BEST: 16.8s — previous 17.4s (session 16, fresh sprint conditions).
Sprint SWOLF 24 matched (INTs 20, 21, 23) — equals session 16 best. Not broken.
Sprint finish consistency: 5 of 6 reps between 16.8–17.3s. Avg 17.15s. Outstanding.
Main set: cramp at 6th 200m rep (50m in). Only 5 full reps completed. 1,050m of planned 1,600m main set.
Cramp trigger: wall push-off + dolphin kick during threshold pace. Recovered with extended rest.
Post-cramp sprint reps: cramp did not recur at ≥2min rest — rest interval is the key variable.
Main set pace 1:38/100m avg — target was 1:33. Rep 1 hit 1:34, drift to 1:41 by rep 4. Normal first-threshold fatigue pattern.
SWOLF drift in main set: 34 → 36 over 5 reps — mild, acceptable.
Garmin HR spike: max 200 bpm in INT 15 (optical artefact). Discard. True max likely ~175 bpm.
Garmin best pace 1:07/100m = INT 20 sprint rep (16.8s / 25m). Confirms sprint time. ✅
Stroke detection OFF + drill log correct = zero mislabeling. ✅
Cool-down completed. Max HR 175 in cool-down — CO2 tolerance still needs work.
Dryland Block 2: quad loading must be controlled, not ballistic. No explosive push-off plyos yet.
sessions.notes: Block 2, Session 1. Threshold pool session. Main set cut short by left quad cramp (50m into rep 6). Sprint finish and cool-down completed after cramp eased. Key result: new sprint protocol best of 16.8s (INT 20) — remarkable given post-cramp, post-threshold fatigue conditions. Sprint finish consistency (5 of 6 reps 16.8–17.3s) is the best sprint set recorded. Stroke detection improvements = clean data throughout. Left quad cramp flags need for controlled quad loading in dryland before returning to explosive push-off work.

sessions.distance_note: Garmin over-counted by 25m due to INT 15 recording a single 25m length as 50m.

metrics.best_25m_split_context: INT 21, clean rep — fresh sprint conditions. Ties session 13 clean best.
metrics.best_25m_unverified_context: INT 15.1 — adjacent Garmin glitch (INT 14) makes timing unreliable. Not counted.
metrics.sprint_set_notes: Rep 1 (INT 14) = 13.5s Garmin glitch — excluded. Rep 2 (INT 15.1) = 16.7s — unverified due to adjacent glitch. Reps 3-10 (INTs 16-23) = reliable data.

coach_flags: NEW SESSION SWOLF BEST: 31 avg (previous 32, session 13).
NEW SPRINT SWOLF BEST: 24 (INT 22) — previous 25 (session 15). Third consecutive improvement: 26→25→24.
NEW AVG PACE BEST: 1:27/100m — previous 1:31 (session 13 and Jan 2026).
Priming worked — main set peaked at 17.4s, finish set averaged 18.2s. Pattern reversed vs session 13.
Sprint protocol best NOT broken on clean reps — 17.0s (session 15) still stands. Today clean best = 17.4s.
Garmin glitches: INT 14 (13.5s butterfly — timing implausible), INT 15 (50m recorded for 25m swum).
Garmin mislabeled INTs 7/8/9 as breaststroke and INTs 14/15.1/16/17/22/37 as butterfly — all were freestyle.
First-length gap in speed bridge: L1 avg 22.3s vs L2 avg 19.7s — consistent 2.5s per rep. Wall push-off primary gap.
Cool-down HR still climbing — INTs 38-39 max HR 153 and 165. CO2 tolerance not improving yet.
Block 1 COMPLETE — this session closes the block.
sessions.notes: Sprint block II of the week. Block 1 closer. Strong session — three new bests (session SWOLF, sprint SWOLF, avg pace). Priming protocol improvements confirmed by main set > finish set pattern reversal. Clean sprint protocol best not broken but SWOLF improvement shows increasing efficiency at same speed. Wall push-off remains primary technical priority for Block 2.

metrics.stroke_drift: 9 avg early → 10-11 late. Fatigue catch collapse.

Cool-down HR 140-163 bpm.

sessions.notes: Technique session. Sprint finish produced protocol bests despite fatigue from 800m main set.

dryland.context: Day after 9/10 sprint session. Fatigue carryover. Baselines should improve fresh.

key_findings: NEW SPRINT PROTOCOL BEST: 16.8s (May 18, session 17, INT 20) — previous 17.0s (session 15). Achieved post-cramp, post-threshold. Also new clean sprint best — previous 17.4s (session 16).
Sprint finish consistency: 5 of 6 reps 16.8–17.3s avg 17.15s — best sprint set recorded.
Sprint SWOLF 24 matched (INTs 20, 21, 23) — equals session 16 all-time best.
Left quad cramp (session 17) at 6th 200m rep — triggered by wall push-off/dolphin kick. Dryland must address quad base before explosive work.
Main set 1:38/100m avg (rep 1 hit 1:34) — target was 1:33. Normal first-threshold fatigue drift.
Stroke detection OFF = clean data throughout. Garmin HR spike to 200 bpm discarded.
SPRINT SWOLF BEST: 24 (May 15, INT 22) — consecutive improvement: 26→25→24 across 3 sprint sessions.
Priming protocol confirmed working — main set peaked over finish set for first time (session 16 vs session 13).
First-length gap in speed bridge: L1 avg 22.3s vs L2 avg 19.7s — wall push-off is primary technical gap.
CO2 tolerance still lagging — cool-down HR peaked at 165–175 bpm across recent sessions.
Block 1 COMPLETE (May 5-15): 3 pool + 1 dryland. Two sprint blocks achieved. Phase 1 targets progressing.

DRYLAND SESSION 18 (May 19): bilateral quad/hip flexor deficit confirmed — right quad pre-cramp in L-sit set 3 (different leg from session 17 cramp). V-ups improved (31 vs 29 baseline). Inverted rows: first baseline 4×12 clean. Pull-ups/dead hang compromised by slippery bars — retest needed.
immediate_priorities: NEXT SESSION: Block 2, Session 3 — Pool/Sprint. Target: break 16.8s clean and fresh. Ensure quad is fully recovered before session.

BILATERAL QUAD FLAG: right quad pre-cramp in L-sit (session 18) confirms general hip flexor endurance deficit. Monitor in pool — if wall push-off triggers any quad sensation on either leg, reduce dolphin kick activation.
BAR HANG EXTERNAL ROTATION: not yet completed — slippery bars forced skip. Carry to Block 3 dryland. Lat pullover replacement still needs a clean session.
PULL-UP RETEST: next dryland, perform pull-ups BEFORE inverted rows on a dry bar to get a valid comparison against session 14 baseline of 22.
HOLLOW BODY: target 25s per set next dryland (matched 20s this session).
L-SIT PROGRESSION: keep at 10-12s sets. Do not push range until 3 full sets complete without pre-cramp sensation.
CO2 TOLERANCE: every-5-stroke cool-down in every pool session. Not yet improving — prioritise completion.
WALL PUSH-OFF DRILL: continue in pool but monitor both quads. Right quad is now flagged alongside left.
